purpose: Auditable source packet for the first real-material Lupine Distill Accuracy publication benchmark.
claim_guardrail: This packet supports paired, stratified, reproducible comparisons. It must not be used to claim universal superiority, DFT-level accuracy, or acceleration without the matching sealed evidence.

material_lanes.why_this_lane_matters: Nickel is a serious EAM/MEAM home-turf system with NIST/OpenKIM provenance and recent surface/defect benchmark coverage. Distill must not hide from a strong classical baseline.
pass_conditions: Classical EAM/MEAM remains competitive on easy fcc Ni bulk mechanics.
Distill Accuracy improves selected MLIP rows or refuses unsafe corrections.
No hidden degradation on easy Ni bulk rows is accepted.

material_lanes.material_family: mixed-bonding oxide or solid ion conductor
material_lanes.why_this_lane_matters: A hard lane prevents the paper from being only a metallic-potential exercise. It tests whether Distill improves or refuses MLIP predictions in regimes where classical EAM/MEAM assumptions may not be scientifically applicable.
pass_conditions: Classical baselines are marked not_applicable when the chemistry makes them invalid.
Distill interventions remain physically coherent across energy, forces, stress, and relaxation stability.
Negative cases are recorded as ribbon evidence.

sources.title: NIST Interatomic Potentials Repository
sources.license: source-specific; cite repository and original potential papers
sources.stewardship: Cite NIST IPR plus every original potential paper and implementation id used.

sources.title: NIST IPR Nickel System Page
sources.license: source-specific; cite repository and original potential papers
sources.stewardship: Use as the auditable index for Ni potential files, implementation ids, DOIs, and notes.

sources.title: OpenKIM: The Open Knowledgebase of Interatomic Models
sources.stewardship: Cite OpenKIM when using model ids, tests, or computed-property records.

sources.title: Evaluation and comparison of classical interatomic potentials through a user-friendly interactive web-interface
sources.license: CC0 for the NIST material data record except where otherwise noted
sources.stewardship: Use for classical potential comparison context and cite the Scientific Data descriptor.

sources.title: Benchmarking 34 OpenKIM Nickel Potentials with an Emphasis on Surfaces and Extended Defects
sources.license: arXiv source license; verify final venue license before reuse
sources.stewardship: Use as the current Ni benchmark framing for bulk, surface, defect, migration, and short-range compression tasks.

sources.title: MS25: Materials Science-Focused Benchmark Data Set for Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
sources.stewardship: Candidate hard-lane source. Before running, choose exact structures, labels, split, and data subset to avoid downloading unnecessary bulk data.

sources.title: Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials are Ready for Solid Ion Conductors
sources.license: arXiv source license; verify data and code license before reuse
sources.stewardship: Candidate Li solid-ion-conductor hard lane. Only use after exact structures, reference labels, and redistribution terms are verified.

sources.title: MLIP Arena: Advancing Fairness and Transparency in Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials via an Open, Accessible Benchmark Platform
sources.license: paper and package licenses must be checked before reuse
sources.stewardship: Use for benchmark-design positioning, especially leakage, fairness, physics-aware metrics, and benchmark surfaces beyond static DFT MAE.

sources.title: Universal machine learning interatomic potentials poised to supplant DFT in modeling general defects in metals and random alloys
sources.license: arXiv source license; verify data and code license before reuse
sources.stewardship: Use for modern defect benchmark context and for positioning complex-defect MLIP claims.

ni_fcc_bulk_elastic_anchor.notes: Elastic constants are the existing NIST-derived atlas-distill Ni scaffold anchor and are used here for source-packet QA, not as a complete publication reference packet.

ni_fcc_lattice_anchor.reference_source: existing atlas-distill Ni LAMMPS trace setup
ni_fcc_lattice_anchor.notes: This is the lattice constant currently used by the existing Ni LAMMPS traces. Before paper submission, replace or supplement with the exact experimental/DFT reference and citation used in the final figure.

ni_fcc_cohesive_anchor.notes: Existing NIST-derived benchmark anchor used by atlas-distill for Ni cohesive energy comparisons.

source_packet: Every source has at least one URL, at least one citation key, a license note, and stewardship instructions.
Every local path referenced for ready evidence exists in the repository.
Every publication candidate has either ready evidence or a status explaining the missing run.
ni_local_baseline: At least three ready Ni classical baselines are present.
At least one MEAM candidate is tracked even if local evidence is not yet generated.
Bulk metrics include C11, C12, C44, a0, and cohesive or relative energy where available.
distill_accuracy: Accuracy comparisons are paired on shared raw prediction checkpoints or seeded trajectories.
Distill does not degrade easy Ni bulk rows without explicit refusal/failure accounting.
Corrections that improve energy while breaking force, stress, or relaxation stability are fault lines, not wins.

next_actions.notes: Sealed release-ready fcc Ni EAM-home-turf fixture covers energy_volume, forces, stress, elastic_constants, and relaxation_stability. Surface, defect, and short-range-compression fixtures still require exact reference-value ingest from open Ni benchmark sources.
